communicative_purpose: To refer to France's high-speed train system
trigger_situations: Train travel, France travel, high-speed rail
contexts: SNCF, French rail system, station announcements
variations: le Train à Grande Vitesse, le TGV, train à grande vitesse
common_mistakes: TGV is specific to French network; other countries have different high-speed names
example_1: Le TGV Paris-Lyon met seulement 2 heures au lieu de 4.
example_2: On prend le TGV pour aller à Marseille, c'est plus rapide.
example_3: Le TGV a une vitesse maximale de 320 kilomètres-heure.
example_1_translation: The Paris-Lyon TGV takes only 2 hours instead of 4.
example_2_translation: We take the TGV to go to Marseille, it’s quicker.
example_3_translation: The TGV has a maximum speed of 320 kilometers per hour.

communicative_purpose: To specify a ticket for both outward and return journeys
trigger_situations: Buying train tickets, travel planning, pricing
contexts: Train stations, ticket counters, online booking
variations: l'allier-retour, le billet retour, le aller-simple
collocates: prix réduit, discount, moins cher, validité
example_1: Le billet aller-retour Paris-Bordeaux coûte moins cher que deux allers simples.
example_2: J'ai acheté un billet aller-retour pour le weekend.
example_3: Vous préférez un billet aller-retour ou juste un aller ?
example_1_translation: The Paris-Bordeaux return ticket costs less than two one-way tickets.
example_2_translation: I bought a return ticket for the weekend.
example_3_translation: Do you prefer a return ticket or just one way?

communicative_purpose: To indicate which platform a train departs from or arrives at
trigger_situations: Station announcements, platform changes, train boarding
variations: le quai, la plateforme, la voie, le rail
common_mistakes: 'Voie' and 'quai' are sometimes confused; 'quai' is the platform you stand on
example_1: Le train pour Nantes part de la voie 4, pas de la 2.
example_2: Attention, changement de voie ! Le train part maintenant du quai 7.
example_3: Toutes les voies sont affichées sur le tableau électronique.
example_1_translation: The train to Nantes leaves from track 4, not 2.
example_2_translation: Be careful, change lanes! The train now departs from platform 7.
example_3_translation: All channels are displayed on the electronic board.

communicative_purpose: To indicate that a train is not arriving or departing on time
trigger_situations: Train delays, travel disruptions, station announcements
contexts: Train stations, rail travel, delays, connections
variations: le train a du retard, le train est annulé, retards
common_mistakes: Confusing with flight delays; 'retard' same in both contexts
collocates: de 15 minutes, cause météo, problèmes techniques
example_1: Le train de 8h est en retard de 20 minutes cause travaux.
example_2: Il y a beaucoup de retards ce matin sur la ligne D.
example_3: Le train a eu 30 minutes de retard à cause de la neige.
example_1_translation: The 8 a.m. train is 20 minutes late due to roadworks.
example_2_translation: There are a lot of delays this morning on line D.
example_3_translation: The train was 30 minutes late due to snow.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a train station
trigger_situations: Travel directions, station references, departure points
contexts: Train stations, railways, city centers, transport hubs
variations: la station, la gare centrale, la gare SNCF, le terminus
common_mistakes: 'Gare' is specifically for trains; 'station' for metro/metro
example_1: La gare de Lyon est à 10 minutes à pied d'ici.
example_2: On se retrouve à la gare, devant le panneau des arrivées.
example_3: La gare de Bordeaux est toute neuve après les travaux.
example_1_translation: Gare de Lyon is a 10-minute walk from here.
example_2_translation: We meet at the station, in front of the arrivals panel.
example_3_translation: Bordeaux station is brand new after the work.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a specific carriage in a train
trigger_situations: Finding seats, carriage numbers, train navigation
variations: la voiture, le wagon, la rame, la carriage
common_mistakes: 'Wagon' can mean freight car in some contexts; 'voiture' is more common for passenger
example_1: Ma place est dans le wagon 12, au wagon 12.
example_2: Les wagons 1 à 6 sont en première classe.
example_3: Le wagon-restaurant est entre les wagons 8 et 9.
example_1_translation: My place is in car 12, in car 12.
example_3_translation: The dining car is between cars 8 and 9.

communicative_purpose: To indicate the raised platform where passengers wait for trains
trigger_situations: Station navigation, platform announcements, waiting for trains
variations: la plateforme, la voie, le quai de départ, quai num
common_mistakes: 'Quai' can also mean quay (harbor); context determines meaning
example_1: Le train pour Lille arrive au quai 2 dans 5 minutes.
example_2: Attendez sur le quai, le train arrive dans 10 minutes.
example_3: Le quai est bondé, beaucoup de monde attend le train.
example_1_translation: The train to Lille arrives at platform 2 in 5 minutes.
example_2_translation: Wait on the platform, the train arrives in 10 minutes.
example_3_translation: The platform is crowded, many people are waiting for the train.

communicative_purpose: To refer to changing trains to continue a journey
contexts: Train stations, rail networks, journey planning
variations: la换乘, le changement, la connexion, le transit
common_mistakes: Confusing with airplane connections; same word used for both
example_1: J'ai 15 minutes de correspondance à Lyon pour le train vers Nice.
example_2: La correspondance pour Paris est au quai 3, en face.
example_3: Si vous manquez la correspondance, le prochain train est dans 2 heures.
example_1_translation: I have a 15 minute connection in Lyon for the train to Nice.
example_2_translation: The connection to Paris is at platform 3, opposite.
example_3_translation: If you miss the connection, the next train is in 2 hours.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the aisle in a train carriage
trigger_situations: Walking through train, seat locations, carriage navigation
variations: l'allée, le couloir central, la galerie, l'aisle
common_mistakes: In trains, 'couloir' is the aisle between seats; 'couloir' can mean hallway
collocates: côté, fenêtre, milieu, aller au couloir
example_1: Ma place est côté couloir, pas côté fenêtre.
example_2: Je suis au milieu du couloir, entre les wagons.
example_3: Le couloir du wagon est étroit quand il y a beaucoup de monde.
example_1_translation: My seat is on the hallway side, not the window side.
example_2_translation: I am in the middle of the corridor, between the cars.
example_3_translation: The carriage corridor is narrow when there are many people.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the distance or time of a journey
trigger_situations: Travel planning, journey duration, route descriptions
variations: le voyage, le parcours, le chemin, la route
common_mistakes: 'Trajet' refers to distance/route; 'voyage' is more about the experience
example_1: Le trajet Paris-Marseille dure environ 3 heures en TGV.
example_2: Quel est le trajet le plus rapide pour aller à Bordeaux ?
example_3: Je fais 45 minutes de trajet chaque jour pour aller au travail.
example_1_translation: The Paris-Marseille journey takes around 3 hours by TGV.
example_2_translation: What is the fastest route to Bordeaux?
example_3_translation: I commute 45 minutes every day to work.

communicative_purpose: To locate power outlets for charging devices on trains
trigger_situations: Charging devices, power outlets, travel amenities
variations: la prise électrique, le prises, le chargeur, l'alimentation
common_mistakes: French plugs are type E; international adapters may be needed
example_1: Il y a une prise électrique près de la fenêtre dans le wagon.
example_2: Je cherche une prise pour charger mon téléphone pendant le trajet.
example_3: Les prises dans le TGV sont entre les sièges.
example_1_translation: There is an electrical outlet near the window in the carriage.
example_2_translation: I'm looking for an outlet to charge my phone during the trip.
example_3_translation: The sockets in the TGV are between the seats.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a reserved seat on a train
variations: la réservation, le booking, la place réservée, confirmer
common_mistakes: English 'booking' used in French; 'réservation' is correct
example_1: J'ai une réservation pour le train de 14h, place 23.
example_2: La réservation est obligatoire pour certains TGV.
example_3: Sans réservation, vous risquez de voyager debout.
example_1_translation: I have a reservation for the 2 p.m. train, seat 23.
example_2_translation: Reservations are required for certain TGVs.
example_3_translation: Without a reservation, you risk traveling standing up.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the person who checks tickets on trains
trigger_situations: Ticket inspection, rail staff, onboard services
contexts: Trains, stations, ticket checks, rail staff
variations: le contrôleur, le chef de train, l'agent, le receveur
common_mistakes: 'Contrôleur' is for trains; 'receveur' is for buses/trams
example_1: Le contrôleur va vérifier vos billets dans 10 minutes.
example_2: J'ai oubliémon billet, le contrôleur va me donner une amende.
example_3: Appelez le contrôleur si vous avez un problème dans le wagon.
example_1_translation: The conductor will check your tickets in 10 minutes.
example_2_translation: I forgot my ticket, the inspector is going to give me a fine.
example_3_translation: Call the conductor if you have a problem in the car.

communicative_purpose: To indicate the final station of a train line or route
trigger_situations: End of line, final destinations, route explanations
variations: le terminus, la station terminus, l'arrêt final,终点站
common_mistakes: English 'terminus' used in French; 'arrêt final' is more common
example_1: Ce train a pour terminus la gare de Lille-Flandres.
example_2: Au terminus, tous les passagers doivent descendre.
example_3: Pour le retour, prenez le métro jusqu'au terminus puis changez.
example_1_translation: This train ends at Lille-Flandres station.
example_2_translation: At the terminus, all passengers must get off.
example_3_translation: To return, take the metro to the terminus then change.

communicative_purpose: To refer to cards that provide discounts on train travel
contexts: SNCF, rail cards, student discounts, senior fares
variations: la carte de fidélité, le pass, la carte giovane, la carte senior
common_mistakes: Multiple card types exist; 'carte de réduction' is general term
example_1: Avec la carte de réduction, vous avez 30% sur le tarif.
example_2: La carte jeune donne des réductions intéressantes pour les 18-27 ans.
example_3: Il vaut mieux prendre la carte de fidélité si vous voyagez souvent.
example_1_translation: With the reduction card, you have 30% off the price.
example_2_translation: The youth card gives attractive discounts for 18-27 year olds.
example_3_translation: It is better to take the loyalty card if you travel often.
